lesson_title: Survival Mode: Stiff Hands vs Strong Dealer

introduction.text: This is the 'Survival Mode'. You have a weak hand (12-16) and I have a STRONG card (7, 8, 9, 10, Ace). You cannot hide. If you Stand, you will likely lose. You must fight. The strategy is scary but simple: You must HIT until you have at least 17.

sequence.text: You have 16. I have 7. If you Stand, I likely have 17 (giving me a win). You are losing anyway. You have to take a chance. Click HIT.

finish.text: Unbelievable! You hit the perfect card. 21 vs my 17. If you had stood, you would have lost.

scenarios.title: Accepting the Risk (15 vs 10)
scenarios.goal: Show that hitting is correct even if you bust.

sequence.text: You have 15. I have 10. This is a desperate situation. Standing is surrender. You must hit and hope for a small card.

finish.text: You busted. It hurts, I know. But look at my hand: I had 18. You would have lost anyway. Hitting was your ONLY chance to win, even if it failed this time.

scenarios.title: Don't be fooled (12 vs 3 vs 12 vs 8)
scenarios.goal: Contrast: 12 vs 2/3 (Hit/Stand depending) vs 12 vs 8 (Always Hit).

sequence.text: You have 12. In the last lesson (vs weak dealer), we stood. But now I have an 8. An 8 is strong. You must assume I have 18. You need to improve. Hit!

finish.text: Good job. You improved to 19 and beat my 17.

scenarios.title: Against the Ace (13 vs A)
scenarios.goal: Playing weak hands against the strongest card.

sequence.text: 13 against Ace. The worst feeling in the world. But math doesn't have feelings. Hit it.
You have 18. That's a respectable score. Now you can Stand.

finish.text: I have 19, so I win. But you fought bravely. 18 is much better than 13.

scenarios.title: The Reward (14 vs 9)
scenarios.goal: Show successful recovery from a bad hand.

sequence.text: 14 vs 9. Danger zone. If you stand, you wait for execution. Hit!

finish.text: Boom! From a weak 14 to a winning 20. This is why we never surrender weak hands against strong dealers.

conclusion.text: You survived the hardest lesson! Hitting on 16 takes guts. Now you know how to handle 'Hard Hands'. In the next lesson, we get a break. We will talk about 'Soft Hands' – hands with an Ace, where you cannot bust!
